title: Praying in Tongues
intro: Of all the gifts and expressions of the Spirit, praying in tongues may be the most misunderstood — and the most underutilized. Gavin's outline gives seven reasons from 1 Corinthians 14 alone for why this gift is vital, essential, and worth exercising throughout the day.
reading:
Gavin draws from 1 Corinthians 14 to show that praying in tongues is not merely a sign or a one-time experience. It is a living, ongoing form of communion with God that carries seven distinct benefits.

First, verse 2 — it is communion with God. One who speaks in a tongue speaks not to men but to God. This is direct, unmediated communion. Second, also verse 2 — it cultivates a deep consciousness of the Spirit's presence. Praying in tongues is a way of living in ongoing awareness that He is here, within, active.

Third, verse 4 — it builds us up. The one who speaks in a tongue edifies himself. Gavin notes specifically that it gives power over ourselves — over the way we think, over our bodies, over our tongues. Fourth, verse 16 — it increases receptivity to knowledge, revelation, teaching, and prophecy. It increases your capacity for the Spirit to illuminate and speak.

Fifth, verse 14 — it exercises the spirit man. The more you pray in the Spirit, the more familiar your spiritual senses become, until they begin to override the merely natural. Sixth, verse 15 — it is spiritual worship. Paul says he sings in the Spirit — and Gavin describes this as one of the most beautiful forms of intimacy with God, bringing deep connection and conscious awareness of His presence.

Seventh, and perhaps most practically — verse 15 — it can be exercised at will. Unlike many gifts, this one is available at any moment, in any place, without a service or a setting. Paul says he prays in the Spirit more than all of them and wishes the same for everyone. This gift is not hindered by circumstance.

Romans 8:26 reminds us that the Spirit Himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words. Praying in tongues is one of the ways we cooperate with that intercession.
reflection: If you pray in tongues, how intentionally and consistently do you exercise this gift throughout the day? If you have not yet received it, have you asked the Father for it with expectation?
abide: Holy Spirit, let my spirit commune with God beyond the reach of my own words. I yield my tongue, my breath, my inner life to You. Intercede through me. Build me up from the inside out. Let prayer be as natural as breathing.
